pulse: US equities closed April at record highs on Thursday, with the S&P 500 breaking above 7,200 for the first time ever - its best monthly performance since 2020 - driven by blowout Alphabet earnings and a VIX collapse. Oil surged to a four-year high on reports the Pentagon is preparing Iran strike options, while the Bank of England held at 3.75% but flagged potential hikes if Middle East conflict stokes inflation. European and Singapore markets are closed today for May Day; US markets open for the final session of the week.

movers.name: VIX
movers.reason: Fear gauge collapsed as S&P set record, signalling broad risk appetite on strong earnings and partial government shutdown resolution

movers.name: DJI
movers.reason: Dow surged as blue-chip earnings and energy stocks rallied on oil jump to highest since 2022

movers.name: Brent
movers.reason: Reports US Central Command prepared short and powerful Iran strike plans sent crude to highest since 2022

movers.name: N225
movers.reason: Nikkei fell against the regional grain, weighed by yen strengthening and cautious Golden Week positioning

portfolio: Strong risk-on day for Marc portfolio as of April 30 close. US positions benefit: S&P hit all-time high above 7,200. IBEX 35 and STI closed higher at +0.78% and +1.06% Thursday, both markets closed for May Day today. Oil holdings gain from Iran fears pushing Brent to $111.42, a four-year high. Gold at $4,642.90 with UBS target $5,900 late 2026. BTC flat around $76,235. EUR/USD at 1.1736 has FX implications for SGD base. 9G2.SI and GCL.SI unavailable today as SGX is closed.

alerts.text: UK terrorism threat raised to SEVERE after Golders Green stabbing. Suspect previously on Prevent watch list.
Oil at 4-year high $111.42 Brent on Iran military strike reports. BoE flagging rates could rise.
Meta slumped on JPMorgan downgrade after AI capex guidance raised - investor patience being tested across big tech.
Nikkei -1.06% only major index in red - yen strength and Golden Week positioning. Japan markets mostly closed next week.
US 76-day government shutdown ended partially on April 30.
S&P 500 above 7,200 for first time ever on April 30. Best monthly gain since 2020.
European and Singapore markets closed today for International Labour Day.

news.title: S&P 500 Closes Above 7,200 for First Time Ever
news.text: US equities surged April 30, S&P 500 hitting record above 7,200, best April since 2020. Alphabet Q1 earnings beat, shutdown resolution, VIX collapse -8.4%. Dow +1.62%, Nasdaq +0.89%.

news.title: Alphabet Q1 Earnings Crush Expectations on AI and Cloud
news.text: Google parent Alphabet Q1 results substantially exceeded Wall Street forecasts on AI expansion and Google Cloud growth, triggering a broad tech rally.

news.title: Meta Slumps After JPMorgan Downgrade on Rising AI Capex
news.text: Meta fell after JPMorgan downgraded citing rising capex guidance that overshadowed solid Q1 results. Divergence from Alphabet cloud returns shaping mega-cap positioning.

news.title: Oil Hits Highest Since 2022 on US Iran Strike Plans
news.text: Brent reached $111.42, highest since 2022, after reports US Central Command developed plans for short and powerful strikes against Iran, following Strait of Hormuz escalation.

news.title: Bank of England Holds 3.75%, Warns of Rate Rises on Iran Inflation
news.text: BoE MPC voted April 30 to hold at 3.75% but flagged Iran conflict inflationary effects could force future hikes. Monitoring Middle East oil and supply chain disruptions closely.

news.title: UK Terrorism Threat Raised to SEVERE After Golders Green Attack
news.text: UK raised threat level to Severe on April 30, first time since November 2021, after Golders Green stabbing. Suspect Essa Suleiman had Prevent referral in 2020 that was subsequently closed.

news.title: US House Votes to End 76-Day Government Shutdown
news.text: Congress passed legislation April 30 restoring most DHS funding, ending 76-day shutdown. Two immigration enforcement subagencies remain unfunded.

news.title: Putin Proposes May 9 Ceasefire; Ukraine Expands Russia Oil Strikes
news.text: Putin proposed temporary ceasefire to Trump for May 9 Victory Day anniversary while Ukraine expanded strikes on Russian oil infrastructure on April 30.

news.title: Israeli Strikes Kill Nine in Lebanon Despite Active Ceasefire
news.text: Israeli operations killed at least nine people including two children in southern Lebanon on April 30 despite active ceasefire agreement, raising durability questions.

news.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Stepping Down, John Ternus Named Successor
news.text: Apple reported extraordinary iPhone demand for Q1 but Tim Cook is stepping down after 15 years as CEO. John Ternus, head of hardware engineering, named as successor.

news.title: Trump Threatens German Troop Cuts Amid Iran Tensions
news.text: Trump signalled potential US military withdrawal from Germany while escalating with Chancellor Merz over Iran policy, injecting NATO eastern flank uncertainty.

news.title: Belgium to Nationalise Nuclear Power Plants
news.text: Belgian PM De Wever announced state takeover of nuclear facilities April 30 to cut fossil fuel dependence amid Middle East price volatility.

news.title: Morgan Stanley: Fed Holds Rates Through All 2026
news.text: Morgan Stanley revised forecast April 30 expecting Fed to hold rates unchanged through all 2026 due to sticky inflation from elevated oil and Iran risk premium.

news.title: UBS Projects Gold at $5,900 by Late 2026
news.text: UBS raised gold target to $5,900 per ounce by late 2026, citing election uncertainty and tariff concerns. Gold closed at $4,642.90 on April 30, implying 27% further upside.

news.title: Singapore Q1 GDP +4.6%; Trade +38.5%; Philippines Carbon Credits Deal
news.text: Singapore Q1 2026 GDP advanced 4.6% YoY. March CPI 1.8%, manufacturing +10.1%, merchandise trade +38.5%. First carbon credits agreement with Philippines signed under Paris Agreement Article 6 on April 30.

day_ahead: Friday May 1, International Labour Day. European markets and SGX closed. US markets open. Key data: April Non-Farm Payrolls consensus around 180,000 jobs; ISM Manufacturing PMI for April. Iran escalation headlines are main market risk. No Fed speakers confirmed. Alphabet earnings to price into European and Asian markets on Monday.

ai_news.title: Anthropic Raising $50B at $900B Valuation
ai_news.text: TechCrunch reported April 29 Anthropic in discussions for $50 billion round at $900 billion valuation, following Google $40 billion commitment announced April 24.

ai_news.title: Musk Testifies xAI Trained Grok Using OpenAI Systems
ai_news.text: Day three of Musk vs OpenAI trial: Musk testified April 30 that xAI trained Grok using OpenAI systems, fuelling the nonprofit-to-for-profit dispute.

ai_news.title: DeepMind David Silver Raises $1.1B for Self-Learning AI
ai_news.text: David Silver, creator of AlphaGo, secured $1.1 billion April 27 for AI systems learning without human training data, a potential paradigm shift beyond supervised fine-tuning.

ai_news.title: OpenAI Rolls Out Yubico Hardware Keys for ChatGPT Security
ai_news.text: OpenAI announced April 30 partnership with Yubico for hardware authentication key support on ChatGPT accounts, addressing enterprise security demand.

ai_news.title: Meta AI Capex Surge Triggers JPMorgan Downgrade
ai_news.text: Meta raised AI capex guidance April 30, JPMorgan downgraded. Contrasts with Alphabet cloud-driven monetisation, crystallising investor divide on AI spending cycles.
